goal: Hide legacy risks and followups from default user-facing signal surfaces while preserving historical audit access

summary.title: Hide historical signals from active queues
summary.what: Changed Mainline's active risk and follow-up queues to materialize only explicit signal-command records, while preserving old seal-summary risks, follow-ups, and anti-patterns as historical intent detail. Added a doctor historical-signals report for old repositories and updated CLI help, Hub wording, README/specs, and AGENTS guidance so default product surfaces teach the explicit signal model.
summary.why: New users should not have to understand earlier seal-embedded signal behavior, and existing old repositories need a non-destructive way to audit old data without letting it pollute default queues or agent context.
summary.user_goal: Hide legacy risks and followups from default user-facing signal surfaces while preserving historical audit access

decisions.chose: Use only explicit MainlineView.Risks and MainlineView.Followups for active queues, Hub risk surfaces, status counts, and seal-prepare applicable items.
decisions.rationale: Seal-summary risks and follow-ups were agent-authored prose from the old model, so keeping them in active queues would preserve the exact noise this change is meant to remove.

decisions.chose: Keep separate historical materializers and surface only aggregate counts through doctor, while leaving detailed old data on source intents for audit.
decisions.rationale: Bulk resolving or deleting old entries would falsely imply they were completed; doctor can explain the hidden history only when a repository actually has it.

decisions.chose: Remove old signal terminology from default risks/followups help, root help, README, Hub search chrome, AGENTS guidance, and protocol/spec descriptions of inherited constraints.
decisions.rationale: The product should teach one model: decisions live in seal, durable constraints/risks/follow-ups are explicit commands, and inherited constraints come only from human-promoted constraints.

review_notes: Validation: go test ./internal/domain; go test ./internal/cli -run '^$'; go test ./internal/engine -run 'Test(MaterializeRisks|MaterializeOpenRisks|MaterializeFollowups|MaterializeOpenFollowups|DoctorReportsHistoricalSealSignals)'; go test ./internal/hub -run 'Test(BuildRiskList|BuildDashboard|HubRiskRadar|HubDecisionHotspots|HubDigest|Export_ProducesAllPageTypes|HubTeamHealth)'.
Dogfood: on this old repository, `go run . risks --json` and `go run . followups --json` now return [], while `go run . doctor --json` reports historical_signals counts for seal-summary data.

A broader `go test ./internal/domain ./internal/engine ./internal/cli ./internal/hub` run was stopped after more than two minutes with only internal/domain completed; focused tests above cover this change without running the heavier engine package path.

architectural_claims: Active risk and follow-up queues are explicit-signal-only surfaces.
Seal-summary risk/follow-up/anti-pattern data is historical audit material, not active work queue input.
Doctor is the maintenance surface for old repositories with historical seal-summary signals.
behavioral_changes: mainline risks and mainline followups no longer list seal-summary risks or follow-ups by default or with --all.
Hub risk lists, dashboards, and risk radar derive risk-bearing rows from explicit open risks and inherited explicit constraints rather than seal-summary fields.
mainline doctor reports historical_signals counts only when old seal-summary signals exist.
Default help and documentation no longer teach users about old signal write paths.

api_changes.signature: mainline risks / mainline followups JSON output

data_model_changes.name: DoctorResult.historical_signals
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Optional diagnostic field appears only when old seal-summary signals exist.

migration_notes: Existing repositories do not need data migration. Old seal-summary signals stop appearing in active queues; doctor surfaces aggregate historical counts for audit.
AGENTS managed template version was bumped to 20 and the repository AGENTS.md block was updated in sync.
